speaker: RIVERS
text: Madam, what makes you in this sudden change?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Why, brother Rivers, are you yet to learn

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: What late misfortune is befall’n King Edward?

speaker: RIVERS
text: What, loss of some pitched battle against Warwick?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: No, but the loss of his own royal person.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Then is my sovereign slain?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ay, almost slain, for he is taken prisoner,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Either betrayed by falsehood of his guard

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Or by his foe surprised at unawares;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And, as I further have to understand,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Is new committed to the Bishop of York,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Fell Warwick’s brother and by that our foe.

speaker: RIVERS
text: These news I must confess are full of grief;

speaker: RIVERS
text: Yet, gracious madam, bear it as you may.

speaker: RIVERS
text: Warwick may lose that now hath won the day.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Till then fair hope must hinder life’s decay;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And I the rather wean me from despair

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: For love of Edward’s offspring in my womb.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: This is it that makes me bridle passion

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And bear with mildness my misfortune’s cross.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Ay, ay, for this I draw in many a tear

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: And stop the rising of blood-sucking sighs,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Lest with my sighs or tears I blast or drown

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: King Edward’s fruit, true heir to th’ English crown.

speaker: RIVERS
text: But, madam, where is Warwick then become?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I am informèd that he comes towards London

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To set the crown once more on Henry’s head.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Guess thou the rest: King Edward’s friends must down.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: But to prevent the tyrant’s violence—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: For trust not him that hath once broken faith—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: I’ll hence forthwith unto the sanctuary

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: To save at least the heir of Edward’s right.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: There shall I rest secure from force and fraud.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: Come, therefore, let us fly while we may fly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELIZABETH
text: If Warwick take us, we are sure to die.
